action: Implement repository-native queue-update corpus discovery at one immutable commit and bind every discovered record to its blob identity before reconciliation.

observed: M-010's gate still accepted a caller-supplied discovery list and parsed record set.
The new runner enumerates committed queue records with git ls-tree and retrieves both bytes and blob identities from the same resolved commit.
inferred: One-commit repository discovery prevents uncommitted, later, or selectively omitted records from silently changing historical queue authority.
proposed: Wire the discovery runner and effective queue gate into CI and autonomous team startup.
superseded: Treating a caller-provided update path list as independent corpus discovery.
Mixing working-tree state with historical queue reconciliation.
unresolved: CI and autonomous startup wiring remain incomplete.
The full production corpus was not executed because this run had no checked-out repository surface.

verification: Three deterministic Node tests passed with zero failures in isolated temporary git repositories, covering complete committed discovery, exclusion of uncommitted files, and historical stability across later commits.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, commit/blob identities, abstract operational state, and synthetic fixtures only; private chat, health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
handoff: Continuity Mining should next add a CI workflow that runs repository-native discovery plus the fail-closed effective queue gate before autonomous queue selection, then persist only accepted output as an artifact.
